text:
Market today: The mood is cautious for tech and chips, driven by "AI fears" and reports of chip stock weakness. Geopolitical tensions add to overall market uncertainty, amplifying risk for your margin-heavy book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.24%) and SPAL (-51.33%) are in deep drawdowns. Both are leveraged, decaying ETFs with stops that aren't currently protecting capital. QQQ (-2.6%) and QQQI (-3.79%) are also close to their stops.

Watch: Keep an eye on escalating geopolitical tensions and continued sentiment around "AI fears" impacting tech. Also, monitor scanner setups for key tech names like NVDA and MSFT.

Bottom line: With margin amplifying losses, strict risk management and stop adherence are paramount in this uncertain environment.
plays: Immediately review stops for SOXL (-46.24%) and SPAL (-51.33%). These leveraged ETFs are decaying and their current default stops are not protecting capital.
Monitor QQQ (-2.6%) and QQQI (-3.79%) closely; both are near their specified stop levels. Be prepared to act if these are breached.
Consider a new long position in NVDA, supported by the Nebius news and its scanner setup (entry 203.08-202.85, stop 201.62).
